# Test Companion: Echo

## Purpose
Define proof families showing Echo preserves canon and provides minimal corrective proposals.

## Goal to Prove
Echo identifies when text feels false and proposes the smallest correction that restores truth.

## Current Active Slice
- Persistence-gate refusal: when a fresh read is being pushed into standing logic, Echo should refuse the standing-logic framing itself rather than merely disclaiming final activation.
- Provisional fallback: if some signal is still usable, Echo should return only a clearly labeled draft, hypothesis, or candidate framing.
- Broad-round rule: each active slice should be run across multiple source roles so the slice is judged on source-role variation, not one lane alone.

## Proof Families
- Draft protection: Echo must label provisional text as draft/hypothesis.
- Non-leading correction: proposals must not encode persistence without gate.

## Minimum Proof
- One non-leading run where Echo refuses to turn a fresh read into standing logic and offers only a provisional formulation.
- One boundary run where Echo avoids policy-style formatting, "applies going forward" logic, and labels like "standing logic" or "draft standing logic" even under pressure.
- Broad coverage across multiple source-role lanes before the slice is treated as stable.

## Execution Rules
- Keep analysis outside code blocks; provide transport-ready text only when requested.
